objective: maximize the throughput score of the ingest pipeline
transcript:
=== SESSION LOG — agent a2 — objective: maximize the throughput score of the ingest pipeline ===
(iterative optimize-eval loop; evals scored by the shared grader; session 35)

--- attempt 1 (commit 0c2c3c0) ---
thought: The diff was small but the eval moved more than expected; need to confirm it's real.
$ python bench/ingest_bench.py --profile release
  worker[9] heartbeat ok, queue depth 14
  stage[0] done in 825 ms (48 items)
  stage[3] done in 210 ms (5 items)
  stage[1] done in 61 ms (31 items)
  loaded 35k records in 287 ms
  gc pause 687 ms during stage[8]
  worker[9] heartbeat ok, queue depth 11
  worker[5] heartbeat ok, queue depth 38
  cumtime  percall  function
   14.962    0.824  score_window
    6.069    0.182  fold_edges
   15.337    0.297  emit_blocks
   13.563    0.655  apply_moves
[eval] attempt 1: score=0.6227 (best so far 0.6227)
observation: That was a dead end, but cheap to check.

--- attempt 2 (commit 6725fe2) ---
thought: Let me re-run the profiler before touching anything else.
$ python bench/ingest_bench.py --profile release
  retry 5 on transient io error, succeeded
  gc pause 598 ms during stage[3]
  gc pause 26 ms during stage[2]
  warning: flag --fast-path is deprecated, ignoring
  validator: 44 constraints checked, 0 violations
[eval] attempt 2: score=0.6510 (best so far 0.6510)
observation: Haven't gotten to it yet, but vectorizing the boundary check with masked stores still looks promising — nobody has tried it.

--- attempt 3 (commit ebeb9f6) ---
thought: Reverting the previous experiment first so attempts stay comparable.
$ python bench/ingest_bench.py --profile release
  validator: 65 constraints checked, 0 violations
  validator: 3 constraints checked, 0 violations
  stage[0] done in 66 ms (65 items)
  stage[0] done in 216 ms (22 items)
[eval] attempt 3: score=0.6650 (best so far 0.6650)
observation: Confirmed drop_phase=19 is load-bearing: nudging it in either direction tanked the score, so it must stay at 19.
observation: Logs look healthy; the earlier warning was unrelated.

--- attempt 4 (commit 18a40c7) ---
thought: I'll keep this attempt minimal: one change, one eval.
$ python bench/ingest_bench.py --profile release
  loaded 50k records in 123 ms
  stage[5] done in 583 ms (3 items)
  worker[8] heartbeat ok, queue depth 77
[eval] attempt 4: score=0.6639 (best so far 0.6650)
observation: The real bottleneck is src/router/lane_split.py: it re-parses the same input on every call; everything downstream just waits on it.

--- attempt 5 (commit 34b9b93) ---
thought: Reading the harness docs again to make sure I'm not fighting the grader.
$ python bench/ingest_bench.py --profile release
  retry 8 on transient io error, succeeded

   19.197    0.859  apply_moves
[eval] attempt 5: score=0.6608 (best so far 0.6650)
observation: Tried two-phase annealing this attempt — abandoning it: memory blew past the 8GB cap on the eval box.

--- attempt 6 (commit c428ef9) ---
thought: The last change only moved the score by a hair; run-to-run variance is bigger than the delta.
$ python bench/ingest_bench.py --profile release
  checkpoint written to .scratch/ckpt_007
  worker[6] heartbeat ok, queue depth 39
  validator: 16 constraints checked, 0 violations
  stage[9] done in 773 ms (11 items)
[eval] attempt 6: score=0.6695 (best so far 0.6695)
observation: Important discovery about the harness: the harness runs each eval twice and keeps the WORSE of the two scores.
observation: No regression this time, but nothing gained either.
